title: Pazzi Conspiracy | Politics and Government | Research Starters
highlights: Pazzi Conspiracy was a significant plot in 15th-century Florence aimed at overthrowing the powerful Medici family.  ...    families like the Pazzi, who sought to regain influence. The conspiracy was orchestrated with the backing of Pope  ...    citizens ran to the Piazza, ignored Jacopo’s cry of “People and liberty!” and rallied around the Medici, shouting “palle, palle,” referring to the balls emblazoned on the Medici coat of arms. The retaliation against the conspirators was swift and brutal. Francesco de’ Pazzi was ignominiously hanged naked from  ...    The houses of the Pazzi and Salviati were sacked. The wealth of the Pazzi was seized, and the Medici and the government of Florence passed laws to obliterate the Pazzi family. All remaining Pazzi had to change their names and their coats of arms. All symbols of the Pazzi, such as their sign of the dolphin, were removed from Florence. The Pazzi name was removed from the tax records
